title: Methods and systems for minimizing haze during holographic recording
abstract: Methods and systems for forming holographic gratings are described herein. The methods and systems may decrease the amount of haze produced during exposure of a holographic recording medium. In some embodiments, the methods and systems include a holographic recording medium; a master hologram containing a grating; and a light source and moveable deflector configured to diffract light through the master hologram into the holographic medium to form a holographic interference pattern. The moveable deflector is configured to move in a direction parallel to the extending direction of the grating. Advantageously, moving the light in this direction allows the holographic interference pattern to remain stationary while there is a spatio-temporal displacement and cancellation of unwanted intensity nonuniformities.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A holographic recording system comprising: a holographic recording medium; and a moveable light source configured to produce at least two intersecting light beams for forming a holographic interference pattern in the holographic recording medium, wherein the moveable light source is configured to move the two intersecting light beams in a direction such that the holographic interference pattern remains stationary however there is spatio-temporal displacement and cancellation of unwanted intensity nonuniformities. 2. The holographic recording system of claim 1 , wherein the moveable light source comprises a grating configured to produce the at least two intersecting light beams. 3. The holographic recording system of claim 1 , wherein the moveable light source comprises a stationary laser with a moveable deflector. 4. The holographic recording system of claim 1 , wherein said at least two intersecting light beams for forming a holographic interference pattern are configured as a scanned beam. 5. The holographic recording system of claim 1 , wherein the moveable light source or the holographic recording medium are configured to translate in a plane parallel to the holographic interference pattern while maintaining stationary exposure angles to perform a line-scan exposure. 6. The holographic recording system of claim 1 , wherein the moveable light source is configured to perform a flood exposure or a selective exposure of the holographic recording medium. -27- 7. A holographic recording system comprising: a holographic recording medium; a master hologram containing a grating; and a light source and moveable deflector configured to diffract light through the master hologram into the holographic medium to form a holographic interference pattern, wherein the moveable deflector is configured to move in a direction parallel to the extending direction of the grating. 8. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the direction is parallel to fringes within the holographic interference pattern. 9. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the moveable deflector comprises a mirror or a lens. 10. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the light source comprises a laser. 11 . The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the moveable deflector is configured to vary least one beam deflection characteristic over the exposure time selected from the group consisting of speed, acceleration, direction, and amplitude. 12. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the moveable deflector is moveable in a pattern comprising a regular waveform or a random waveform. 13. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the moveable deflector is further configured to move along fringes within the holographic interference pattern with magnitude less than or equal to 2 degrees. 14. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the holographic recording material is selected from a group consisting of a holographic photopolymer, a mixture of liquid crystal (LC) and monomer and a mixture of nanoparticles and monomer. 15. The holographic recording system of claim 7, wherein the beam deflector is configured to tune a grating formation process by varying at least one beam deflection characteristic selected from the group consisting of speed, acceleration, direction, and amplitude. 16. A method for producing a holographic grating, the method comprising: providing a holographic recording medium; exposing the holographic recording medium with a moveable light source configured to produce at least two intersecting laser beams for forming a holographic interference pattern in the holographic recording medium; moving the at least two intersecting laser beams in a direction that leaves the holographic interference pattern stationary and results in a spatio-temporal displacement and cancellation of unwanted intensity nonuniformities. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the moveable light source comprises a grating configured to produce the at least two intersecting light beams. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the moveable light source comprises a stationary laser with a moveable deflector. 19. The method of claim 16, wherein said at least two intersecting light beams for forming a holographic interference pattern are configured as a scanned beam. 20. The method of claim 16, further comprising translating the moveable light source or the holographic recording medium in a plane parallel to the holographic interference pattern while maintaining stationary exposure angles to perform a line-scan exposure. 21. The method of claim 16, wherein exposing the holographic recording medium comprises a flood exposure or a selective exposure of the holographic recording medium. 22. A method for producing a holographic grating, the method comprising: providing a holographic recording medium; providing a master hologram containing a grating; exposing the holographic recording medium with a light source and moveable deflector configured to diffract light through the master hologram and into the holographic medium to form a holographic interference pattern; and moving the moveable deflector in a direction parallel to the extending direction of the grating. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the direction is parallel to fringes within the holographic interference pattern. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the moveable deflector comprises a mirror or a lens. 25. The method of claim 22, wherein the light source comprises a laser. 26. The method of claim 22, wherein moving the moveable deflector comprises varying at least one beam deflection characteristic over an exposure time selected from the group consisting of speed, acceleration, direction, and amplitude. 27. The method of claim 22, wherein the moveable deflector is moveable in a pattern comprising a regular waveform or a random waveform. 28. The method of claim 22, wherein moving the moveable deflector comprises moving along fringes within the holographic interference pattern with magnitude less than or equal to 2 degrees. 29. The method of claim 22, wherein the holographic recording material is selected from a group consisting of a holographic photopolymer, a mixture of liquid crystal (LC) and monomer and a mixture of nanoparticles and monomer. -SO- 30. The method of claim 22, wherein moving the beam deflector comprises tuning to stages in a grating formation process at least one beam deflection characteristic over an exposure time selected from the group consisting of speed, acceleration, direction, and amplitude. 31 . A holographic line scan exposure system comprising: a holographic recording medium; a moveable light source configured to produce at least two intersecting light beams for forming a holographic interference pattern in the holographic recording medium, the holographic interference pattern having an extending direction; and an optical element for providing an exposure beam convergence or divergence in a plane both containing the extending direction and substantially orthogonal to the surface of the holographic recording medium, wherein the moveable light source or the holographic recording medium are configured to translate in a plane parallel to the extending direction while maintaining stationary exposure angles such that the holographic interference pattern remains stationary, wherein the combination of the exposure beam convergence or divergence by the optical element and the translation of the movable light source or the holographic recording medium provide spatio-temporal displacement and cancellation of unwanted intensity nonuniformities. 32. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein the holographic recording medium is applied to a curved substrate. 33. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein the optical element includes at least one cylindrical surface. 34. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein the optical element includes at least one freeform surface. -31 - 35. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein the intersecting light beam directions produced by the movable light source lie in a plane substantially orthogonal to the plane within which the beam convergence or divergence by the optical element takes place. 36. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 35, wherein deviations from orthogonality between the plane containing the intersecting light beam directions produced by the movable light source and the plane within which the beam convergence or divergence by the optical element takes place are less than 2 degrees. 37. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein the holographic interference pattern comprises fringes curved in at least one dimension and wherein the extending direction is along a tangent to at least a portion of the fringes. 38. The holographic line scan exposure system of claim 31 , wherein deviations from orthogonality between the plane within which beam convergence or divergence by the optical element takes place and the surface of the holographic recording medium are less than 2 degrees. -32-
